lastSeen: June 30, 1994, near her home in South Los Angeles, CA

name: Dr. John Bayard Britton
summary: Abortion provider Dr. John Britton and his clinic escort James Barrett were shot dead outside a Pensacola clinic by anti-abortion activist Paul Jennings Hill. Hill was convicted and executed in 2003, the first person executed for anti-abortion violence in the US.
lastSeen: July 29, 1994, Ladies Center clinic, Pensacola, FL

name: Hawaii Jane Doe (1994)
summary: An unidentified woman's remains were found on Maui, Hawaii in March 1994. Despite forensic examination, she has never been identified. Her case remains in Hawaii's NamUs entries.

Lamar Johnson was wrongfully convicted and spent 28 years in prison before being exonerated in 2023 after the St. Louis Circuit Attorney's office found evidence of a corrupt investigation. Johnson's exoneration highlighted prosecutorial misconduct in St. Louis.

name: Mary Alice Livingston
summary: Forty-one-year-old Mary Alice Livingston was found murdered in Eugene, Oregon in December 1994. Despite investigation by Eugene Police, the case went cold. It remains in Lane County cold case files.

name: Megan Kanka

Megan's Law — which requires community notification when sex offenders live in a neighborhood — was passed in her memory. Timmendequas was convicted and sentenced to death, later commuted.

name: Melissa Witt
summary: Nineteen-year-old Melissa Witt was abducted from a bowling alley parking lot in Fort Smith, Arkansas. Her body was found weeks later in a forest. Despite decades of investigation, her killer has never been identified.
lastSeen: December 1, 1994, bowling alley parking lot, Fort Smith, AR

name: Nicole Brown Simpson & Ron Goldman
summary: Nicole Brown Simpson and Ron Goldman were stabbed to death outside her Brentwood condominium. Nicole's ex-husband O.J. Simpson was tried for the murders in the 'Trial of the Century' and acquitted in 1995. He was later found liable in civil court. The criminal case was never solved.
lastSeen: June 12, 1994, Bundy Drive, Brentwood, Los Angeles

name: Nicole Brown Simpson & Ron Goldman (OJ)
summary: Nicole Brown Simpson (35) and Ron Goldman (25) were stabbed to death outside Nicole's condominium. O.J. Simpson was tried and acquitted in the most-watched trial in TV history. He was later found liable in civil court. The case changed American attitudes about celebrity, race, and the justice system.
lastSeen: June 12, 1994, 875 S. Bundy Drive, Brentwood, CA

name: Pine Ridge Jane Doe
summary: An unidentified Native American woman's remains were found on the Pine Ridge Reservation in South Dakota in September 1994. She has never been identified. Her case is in NamUs.

name: Deanna Cremin

lastSeen: March 31, 1995, leaving a party in Somerville, MA

name: Jodi Huisentruit

lastSeen: June 27, 1995, parking lot near her apartment, Mason City, IA

Questions remain about possible additional conspirators, and this was the deadliest act of domestic terrorism until 9/11.

name: Stephanie Nolen
summary: Eighteen-year-old Stephanie Nolen was found murdered in Denver, Colorado in June 1995. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Denver County cold case files.

name: Amber Hagerman
summary: Nine-year-old Amber Hagerman was abducted while riding her bicycle in an Arlington parking lot. A neighbor saw a man pull her into his truck. Her body was found four days later in a drainage ditch. Her kidnapping directly inspired the creation of the AMBER Alert system, which has since saved thousands of children.
lastSeen: January 13, 1996, Winn-Dixie parking lot, Arlington, TX

name: Amber Hagerman (AMBER Alert Origin)
summary: Nine-year-old Amber Hagerman was abducted and murdered in Arlington, Texas, directly inspiring the creation of the AMBER Alert system. Her killer has never been identified despite decades of investigation. The AMBER Alert system has since saved thousands of children across America.

name: Anne Marie Fahey
summary: Governor's scheduler Anne Marie Fahey disappeared from Wilmington, Delaware. Prominent attorney Thomas Capano was convicted of her murder after her body was found at sea. Capano had thrown her body overboard in a cooler. He was initially sentenced to death, later commuted to life.

name: Brenda Robinson

name: Cayce Lynn McDaniel

A ransom note demanding $118,000 was found, but JonBenét had never left the house. She had been struck on the head and strangled. Despite extensive investigation involving thousands of suspects, no arrests have ever been made.
lastSeen: December 25, 1996, at home in Boulder, CO

name: JonBenét Ramsey (Renewed Investigation)
summary: JonBenét Ramsey's case has been periodically reopened. In 2023, Boulder police announced they were working with a company using advanced DNA technology to reanalyze evidence. A profile of an unidentified male was enhanced, potentially leading to new suspects. The case remains officially unsolved after nearly 30 years.
lastSeen: December 25, 1996, family home in Boulder, CO

name: Kristin Smart
summary: Cal Poly freshman Kristin Smart disappeared after a party near campus. Fellow student Paul Flores was the last person seen with her. After 25 years as a cold case, Flores was arrested in 2021 and convicted of first-degree murder in 2022. Kristin's body was never recovered despite extensive searches.
lastSeen: May 25, 1996, near Cal Poly campus, San Luis Obispo, CA

name: Patsy Ramsey as Suspect
summary: The JonBenét Ramsey case has evolved over decades. DNA testing in 2008 exonerated the Ramsey family. Advanced touch DNA analysis in 2023 created a new profile of an unknown male. The case remains one of America's most discussed unsolved murders with no arrest after nearly 30 years.

name: Rebecca Williams
summary: Twenty-seven-year-old Rebecca Williams was found murdered in Dover, Delaware. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Delaware State Police cold case files.

name: Robin Carver

name: Scott Hamlin & Lollie Winans

Randall Lee Smith, who had committed previous park murders, was eventually convicted. The case highlighted dangers in national parks.
lastSeen: May 1996, Appalachian Trail, Shenandoah National Park, VA

Despite numerous witnesses and theories implicating rival gang members and even law enforcement, no one was ever charged with his murder.
lastSeen: September 7, 1996, MGM Grand Garden Arena, Las Vegas, NV

name: Tupac Shakur (Las Vegas)
summary: Tupac Shakur was shot four times in a drive-by shooting while stopped at a red light in Las Vegas after attending a Mike Tyson boxing match. He died of his injuries six days later. Despite numerous witnesses and suspects, no one has ever been charged. The case remains officially open by Las Vegas Metro Police.
lastSeen: September 7, 1996, Flamingo Road, Las Vegas, NV

name: Amy Bechtel

lastSeen: July 24, 1997, mountain road near Lander, WY

lastSeen: January 16, 1997, I-405 near Bel Air, Los Angeles, CA

Cunanan had already killed four others across the country before targeting Versace. He killed himself on a houseboat eight days after the Versace murder. The case was dramatized in 'The Assassination of Gianni Versace.'
lastSeen: July 15, 1997, his mansion steps at Ocean Drive, Miami Beach, FL

name: Kristen Modafferi
summary: Eighteen-year-old Kristen Modafferi disappeared from San Francisco while working a summer job before starting at UC San Diego. She was last seen on a bus. Despite nationwide searches and continued investigation, Kristen has never been found.

name: Michelle Moore-Bosko
summary: Eighteen-year-old Michelle Moore-Bosko was raped and murdered in her Norfolk apartment. Four Navy sailors — the 'Norfolk Four' — were coerced into false confessions and convicted. Another man, Danial Williams, eventually confessed. Four of the men were exonerated after years of advocacy. The case exposed problems with coerced confessions.
lastSeen: July 8, 1997, her apartment in Norfolk, VA

name: Paul Brooks

lastSeen: January 1997, family home in Hilo, HI

name: The Notorious B.I.G. (Christopher Wallace)

He was killed just six months after Tupac Shakur. Despite a massive investigation and multiple civil lawsuits, no one has ever been charged with his murder.
lastSeen: March 9, 1997, Petersen Automotive Museum, Los Angeles, CA

John William King and Lawrence Russell Brewer were executed; Shawn Berry received a life sentence. The brutal hate crime led to the James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Act.

name: Paul Scagnetti
summary: Forty-four-year-old Paul Scagnetti was found murdered in Las Vegas, Nevada in April 1998. Despite investigation by Las Vegas Metro Police, the case went cold. It remains in Clark County cold case files.

name: Shawn Berry Release
summary: The brutal 1998 murder of James Byrd Jr. in Jasper, Texas led to convictions of all three perpetrators. John King was executed in 2019. Lawrence Brewer was executed in 2011. Shawn Berry received life imprisonment and is eligible for parole. The case inspired federal hate crime legislation.

name: Stacy Wilson

Her bookbag was found buried in a construction site 26 miles away over a year later. Despite extensive searches and investigations, Asha has never been found and the case remains open.

lastSeen: May 4, 2001, Vitello's Restaurant, Studio City, Los Angeles

name: Bonny Lee Bakley Murder
summary: Bonny Lee Bakley was shot dead while sitting in Robert Blake's car outside a restaurant in Studio City. Blake was acquitted in criminal court in 2005 but found liable in civil court. No other suspect was charged and the case remains officially unsolved. Blake died in 2023.
lastSeen: May 4, 2001, Vitello's Restaurant, Studio City, CA

name: Duran Bailey
summary: Duran Bailey was found murdered in Las Vegas in 2001. Kirstin Blaise Lobato was convicted of his murder but her conviction was vacated after innocence advocates showed she was 200 miles away. A retrial led to a plea deal. The true killer was never identified.

name: Henryk Siwiak
summary: Henryk Siwiak, a Polish immigrant, was shot and killed in Brooklyn on the same night as the 9/11 attacks. Because all police resources were diverted to the World Trade Center, the crime scene was not properly processed. His murder remains the only unsolved homicide committed in New York on September 11, 2001.

name: Jennifer Mazzini
summary: Twenty-one-year-old Jennifer Mazzini disappeared from Egg Harbor Township, New Jersey in April 2001. She was last seen leaving a casino. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains in Atlantic County cold case files.
lastSeen: April 6, 2001, near Tropicana Casino, Atlantic City area, NJ

name: Jill-Lyn Euto
summary: College student Jill-Lyn Euto was found stabbed to death in her Syracuse, New York apartment in May 2001. No arrests were made in the case. Her murder remains open with Syracuse Police.
lastSeen: May 2001, her apartment near Syracuse University, NY

name: Kent Heitholt
summary: Columbia Tribune sports editor Kent Heitholt was beaten and strangled in the Tribune parking lot. Two men were convicted — Ryan Ferguson and Charles Erickson — but Ferguson's conviction was vacated in 2013 after key witness recanted. Erickson's conviction stands but the case has become emblematic of wrongful conviction concerns.
lastSeen: November 1, 2001, Columbia Tribune parking lot, Columbia, MO

name: Maria Jones
summary: Twenty-five-year-old Maria Jones was found murdered in Baton Rouge, Louisiana in May 2001. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in East Baton Rouge Parish cold case files.

name: Michelle Martinez
summary: Twenty-nine-year-old Michelle Martinez was found murdered in Phoenix, Arizona in April 2001. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Maricopa County cold case files.

name: Sandy Forman
summary: Twenty-nine-year-old Sandy Forman was found murdered in Tampa, Florida in March 2001. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office cold case files.

name: Stephanie Eldredge
summary: Thirty-five-year-old Stephanie Eldredge was found murdered in Boise, Idaho in September 2001. Despite investigation by Boise Police, the case went cold. It remains in Ada County cold case files.

name: Thomas C. Wales
summary: Federal prosecutor Thomas Wales, also known for his gun control advocacy, was shot through the window of his home-office in Seattle by a single bullet. A suspect was identified but never charged due to insufficient evidence. His murder remains one of only two unsolved killings of a federal prosecutor in US history.
lastSeen: October 11, 2001, his home in Seattle, WA

name: Tom Wells
summary: Forty-two-year-old Tom Wells was found murdered in Charlotte, North Carolina in August 2001. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Mecklenburg County cold case files.

name: Alexis Patterson
summary: Seven-year-old Alexis Patterson disappeared near her Milwaukee school on the same day Elizabeth Smart was abducted in Utah. Alexis's disappearance received far less media coverage, sparking national conversation about racial disparities in missing children coverage. She has never been found.
lastSeen: May 3, 2002, near Hi-Mount School, Milwaukee, WI

name: Andrea Soos
summary: Nineteen-year-old Andrea Soos was found murdered in Lancaster, Pennsylvania in December 2002. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Lancaster County cold case files.

name: Ariel Castro Victims
summary: Michelle Knight, Amanda Berry, and Gina DeJesus were individually abducted and held captive for about a decade in Ariel Castro's Cleveland home. Amanda Berry escaped in 2013 with her daughter born in captivity. Castro pleaded guilty to 937 counts and received life imprisonment plus 1,000 years. He died by suicide in prison one month later.

name: Daniel Pearl
summary: Wall Street Journal reporter Daniel Pearl was kidnapped and murdered in Pakistan in 2002. Omar Sheikh was convicted in Pakistan. Ahmed Omar Saeed Sheikh's death sentence was overturned in 2021 by Pakistan's Supreme Court. The case highlighted dangers faced by journalists in conflict zones.

name: Danielle van Dam
summary: Seven-year-old Danielle van Dam was abducted from her Sabre Springs home and murdered by neighbor David Westerfield. Her body was found in a rural area two months later. Westerfield was convicted and sentenced to death. He remains on California death row.
lastSeen: February 1, 2002, family home in Sabre Springs, CA

name: Elizabeth Smart
summary: Fourteen-year-old Elizabeth Smart was abducted from her Salt Lake City bedroom by Brian David Mitchell and his wife. She was held captive for nine months, often in plain sight. She was rescued after being recognized in public. Mitchell was convicted and sentenced to life in prison in 2011.
lastSeen: June 5, 2002, family home in Salt Lake City, UT

name: Erin Runnion
summary: Five-year-old Samantha Runnion was abducted from outside her Orange County apartment complex and murdered. Her mother Erin Runnion became a prominent child safety advocate, founding the Joyful Child Foundation. Alejandro Avila was convicted and sentenced to death in 2005.
lastSeen: July 15, 2002, outside her apartment in Stanton, CA

name: Jennifer Graves
summary: Twenty-five-year-old Jennifer Graves was found murdered in Tacoma, Washington in March 2002. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Pierce County cold case files.

name: Jennifer Servo
summary: Television news reporter Jennifer Servo was found beaten and strangled in her Abilene apartment. She worked at a local TV station and had recently started a new relationship. Suspects included an ex-boyfriend and a coworker, but no arrests have ever been made.
lastSeen: October 14, 2002, her apartment in Abilene, TX

name: John Gilbride
summary: John Gilbride, involved in a bitter custody dispute with members of the MOVE organization, was shot and killed in his car in Maple Shade, New Jersey. Despite the obvious motive and suspects, no one was ever charged with his murder. The case highlighted the controversy surrounding MOVE.
lastSeen: September 27, 2002, parking lot in Maple Shade, NJ

name: Laci Peterson
summary: Laci Peterson was eight months pregnant when she disappeared on Christmas Eve. Her husband Scott Peterson reported her missing and became the prime suspect. Laci's remains and those of her unborn son Conner washed ashore in April 2003. Scott Peterson was convicted of first-degree murder in 2004 and sentenced to death.
lastSeen: December 24, 2002, family home in Modesto, CA

name: Laci Peterson (Detailed)
summary: Eight-months-pregnant Laci Peterson vanished on Christmas Eve. Her husband Scott reported her missing and became the prime suspect. Laci's remains and those of her unborn son washed ashore near the San Francisco Bay. Scott Peterson was convicted of first-degree murder of Laci and second-degree murder of their unborn son Conner.
lastSeen: December 24, 2002, family home in Modesto, CA

name: Rachel Louise Cooke
summary: Nineteen-year-old Rachel Cooke went for a morning jog near her parents' home during winter break from college and never returned. A neighbor saw a truck driving slowly behind her. Despite extensive searches and a $100,000 reward, Rachel has never been found.
lastSeen: January 10, 2002, jogging near family home, Georgetown, TX

name: Sara Ann Lewis Trusty
summary: Sara Ann Trusty disappeared while biking near her church in the Galveston County area. Her body was found days later in a local reservoir. Despite investigation, the killer has never been identified or arrested.
lastSeen: March 17, 2002, near her church in the Texas City area, TX

name: Sarah Martin
summary: Twenty-seven-year-old Sarah Martin was found murdered in Denver, Colorado in August 2002. Despite investigation by Denver Police, the case went cold. It remains in Denver cold case files.

name: Shawn Hornbeck
summary: Eleven-year-old Shawn Hornbeck was abducted while riding his bike in Richwoods, Missouri. He was held captive for four and a half years by Michael Devlin in a nearby town. Police found him while searching for another missing boy, Ben Ownby. Devlin pleaded guilty and received multiple life sentences.
lastSeen: October 6, 2002, near his home in Richwoods, MO

name: Wendy Carr
summary: Seventeen-year-old Wendy Carr disappeared from Waterbury, Connecticut in June 2002. She was last seen leaving a friend's home. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains in New Haven County cold case files.

name: Wendy Crawford
summary: Thirty-year-old Wendy Crawford disappeared from Tacoma, Washington in November 2002. She was last seen leaving a shopping center. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains in Pierce County Sheriff's Office files.

name: Blue Ridge Savings Bank Murders
summary: Three bank employees were found dead in the utility room of a Blue Ridge Savings Bank in Greer, South Carolina. Sylvia Holtzclaw, James 'Eb' Barnes, and Margaret 'Maggie' Barnes were all killed. The bank had recently closed and was being vacated. Despite investigation, no one has been charged.
lastSeen: December 5, 2003, Blue Ridge Savings Bank, Greer, SC

name: Bonnie Garza
summary: Twenty-seven-year-old Bonnie Garza was found murdered in El Paso, Texas in April 2003. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in El Paso County cold case files.

name: Cynthia Anderson
summary: Twenty-seven-year-old Cynthia Anderson was found murdered in Mobile, Alabama in September 2003. Despite investigation by Mobile Police, the case went cold. It remains in Mobile County cold case files.

name: Dru Sjodin
summary: University of North Dakota student Dru Sjodin was abducted from a mall parking lot in Grand Forks by registered sex offender Alfonso Rodriguez Jr. Her body was found in Minnesota. Rodriguez was sentenced to death, making it a federal case that crossed state lines.
lastSeen: November 22, 2003, Columbia Mall parking lot, Grand Forks, ND

name: Jason Johnson (Camoflauge)
summary: Rapper Jason Johnson, known as Camoflauge, was shot and killed in Savannah, Georgia near his car. He was found by his young daughter. Despite a reward offered for information, his murder remains unsolved. He was known for his hometown pride and street-level storytelling in his music.
lastSeen: April 3, 2003, near his car in Savannah, GA

name: Joyce Weirshiem
summary: Forty-eight-year-old Joyce Weirshiem was found murdered in Henderson, Nevada in April 2003. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Henderson Police Department cold case files.

name: Linda Sobieraj
summary: Linda Sobieraj was found murdered in her Indianapolis home in March 2003. Despite investigation by Indianapolis Metro Police, no arrests were made and the case went cold.
lastSeen: March 19, 2003, her home in Indianapolis, IN

name: Mary Alice Burnside
summary: Thirty-two-year-old Mary Alice Burnside was found murdered in Shreveport, Louisiana in November 2003. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Caddo Parish cold case files.

name: Patricia Harris
summary: Forty-four-year-old Patricia Harris was found murdered in Raleigh, North Carolina in February 2003. Despite investigation by Raleigh Police, the case went cold. It remains under investigation.

name: Paul Johnson
summary: Forty-one-year-old Paul Johnson was found murdered in Minneapolis, Minnesota in September 2003. Despite investigation by Minneapolis Police, the case went cold. It remains in Hennepin County cold case files.

name: Tamara Greene
summary: Tamara Greene, known as 'Strawberry,' was an exotic dancer found shot to death in her car in Detroit. Theories linked her death to a party she allegedly attended at the Manoogian Mansion involving Mayor Kwame Kilpatrick. Despite investigations and a lawsuit by her family, no arrests were ever made.

name: Terrence Williams & Felipe Santos
summary: Terrence Williams and Felipe Santos both separately disappeared after being given rides by Collier County Sheriff's Deputy Steve Calkins, who dropped them at their destinations according to his account. Neither man was ever seen again. Calkins was fired but never criminally charged. The families believe the men were murdered.

name: Todd Kohlhepp Victims
summary: Real estate agent Todd Kohlhepp was identified as a serial killer after a chained survivor was found on his rural South Carolina property in 2016. He confessed to seven murders including the unsolved 2003 Superbike Motorsports shooting. He was sentenced to seven consecutive life terms.

name: Brendan Tevada
summary: Seventeen-year-old Brendan Tevada disappeared from Wichita, Kansas in July 2004. Despite investigation, he was never found. His case remains open in Sedgwick County Sheriff's Office files.

name: Brianna Maitland
summary: Seventeen-year-old Brianna Maitland disappeared after her car was found abandoned at a farmhouse in Montgomery, Vermont. She was never found and the case has never been solved despite active investigation over two decades.

name: Carlie Brucia
summary: Eleven-year-old Carlie Brucia was abducted from a car wash in Sarasota on Super Bowl Sunday while walking home. Surveillance footage of her abduction was broadcast nationally. Joseph P. Smith was convicted of her murder and sentenced to death. Her case led to the passage of 'Carlie's Law' in Florida.
lastSeen: February 1, 2004, Evie's Car Wash, Sarasota, FL

name: Carrie Ann Lucas
summary: Carrie Ann Lucas was found murdered in Houston, Texas in September 2004. Her case went cold despite investigation by Houston Police Department. Her family has continued to advocate for answers.

name: Dana Clair Edwards
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Dana Clair Edwards disappeared from Lexington, South Carolina in March 2004. She was last seen leaving a bar. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains open.

name: Donna Hall
summary: Thirty-four-year-old Donna Hall was found murdered in Richmond, Virginia in July 2004. Despite investigation by Richmond Police, the case went cold. It remains in Henrico County cold case files.

name: Lori Erickson
summary: Twenty-one-year-old Lori Erickson disappeared after leaving a party near Iowa State University in Ames. Despite a large-scale search, she was never found. Her case received media attention and remains open.
lastSeen: November 22, 2004, near Iowa State University, Ames, IA

name: Maura Murray
summary: UMass Amherst student Maura Murray crashed her car on a remote road in New Hampshire and vanished before help arrived. A witness offered assistance but she declined. Despite extensive searches and one of the most followed internet investigations in cold case history, Maura has never been found.

name: Monica Dottore
summary: Twenty-eight-year-old Monica Dottore was found murdered in Hampton, Virginia in August 2004. Despite investigation by Hampton Police, the case went cold. It remains in Hampton City cold case files.

name: Paul Cox
summary: Thirty-seven-year-old Paul Cox was found murdered in Trenton, New Jersey in October 2004. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Mercer County cold case files.

name: Sarah Fox
summary: Twenty-five-year-old Sarah Fox was killed while jogging near her Minneapolis home. Her body was found in the Mississippi River gorge. Michael Zebuhr's DNA was not a match but DNA evidence eventually pointed to Nathaniel Turner who pleaded guilty in 2008.
lastSeen: May 9, 2004, near her home in Minneapolis, MN

name: Albuquerque I-40 Shooter Victims
summary: Multiple motorists were shot at along Interstate 40 near Albuquerque in 2005. One person died and several were injured. Despite the crimes occurring on a major interstate, the shooter was never identified.

name: Eric Pais
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Eric Pais was found murdered in Las Vegas, Nevada in March 2005. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Clark County cold case files.

name: Jeff Davis 8 Victims
summary: Eight women and girls were found dead in and around Jefferson Davis Parish over several years. Many were involved in prostitution or drug use. The FBI investigated allegations that a local drug ring involving law enforcement was responsible for some deaths. No one has been charged with any of the eight murders.
lastSeen: Various dates, Jefferson Davis Parish, LA

name: Jennifer Williams
summary: Thirty-one-year-old Jennifer Williams disappeared from Louisville, Kentucky in September 2005. She was last seen leaving a bar. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains open with Louisville Metro Police.

name: Jessica Lunsford
summary: Nine-year-old Jessica Lunsford was abducted from her Homosassa home and murdered by registered sex offender John Couey. Her remains were found in a bag buried near Couey's trailer. She had been buried alive. Couey was convicted and sentenced to death but died in prison of natural causes. Jessica's Law was passed in her memory.
lastSeen: February 24, 2005, family home in Homosassa, FL

name: Michaela Shamblin
summary: Twenty-two-year-old University of Iowa student Michaela Shamblin was reported missing in February 2005. She was last seen leaving a bar. Despite extensive investigation, she was never found. Her case remains an ongoing investigation.

name: Natalee Holloway
summary: Alabama teenager Natalee Holloway disappeared while on a graduation trip to Aruba. She was last seen leaving a bar with Joran van der Sloot. Joran was repeatedly investigated but never charged in her disappearance. He later murdered another woman in Peru and is serving time there. Natalee was declared legally dead in 2012.
lastSeen: May 30, 2005, Carlos'n Charlie's bar, Oranjestad, Aruba

name: Rachel Davey
summary: Twenty-four-year-old Rachel Davey was found murdered in Stafford County, Virginia. She was pregnant. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Stafford County cold case files.

name: Tamara Lee Bey
summary: Thirty-one-year-old Tamara Lee Bey was found murdered in Newark, New Jersey in July 2005. Despite investigation by Newark Police, the case went cold. It remains in Essex County cold case files.

name: Teresa Halbach
summary: Freelance photographer Teresa Halbach disappeared after photographing a vehicle at the Avery Salvage Yard. Her remains were found on the property. Steven Avery and his nephew Brendan Dassey were both convicted of murder. The Netflix documentary 'Making a Murderer' brought global attention to the case, raising questions about the investigation.
lastSeen: October 31, 2005, Avery Salvage Yard, Manitowoc County, WI

name: Victoria Anne Banks
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Victoria Anne Banks was found murdered in Tuscaloosa, Alabama in August 2005. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Tuscaloosa County cold case files.

name: Amber Hunt
summary: Nineteen-year-old Amber Hunt was found murdered in Cincinnati, Ohio in August 2006. Despite investigation by Cincinnati Police, the case went cold. It remains in Hamilton County cold case files.

name: Billy Meyers
summary: Nineteen-year-old Billy Meyers disappeared from Cedar Rapids, Iowa in April 2006 after a night out. He was never found. His case is linked to a series of similar disappearances of young men from Midwest college towns near rivers.

name: Bobbie Jean Chambers
summary: Thirty-one-year-old Bobbie Jean Chambers was found murdered in Charleston, West Virginia in October 2006. Despite investigation by Charleston Police, the case went cold. It remains in Kanawha County cold case files.

name: Cassie Jo Stoddart
summary: Sixteen-year-old Cassie Jo Stoddart was murdered while alone housesitting in Pocatello, Idaho. Brian Draper and Torey Adamcik were convicted of her murder. They had secretly filmed themselves discussing plans to become serial killers before carrying out the attack.

name: Hannah Harris
summary: Seventeen-year-old Hannah Harris, a member of the Osage Nation, disappeared from Pawhuska, Oklahoma in September 2006. She was last seen leaving a party. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case is part of the documented MMIW crisis.

name: Jennifer Kesse
summary: Twenty-four-year-old Jennifer Kesse disappeared from her Orlando condominium complex. Surveillance footage captured an unidentified person driving her car. Despite national media coverage and a $125,000 reward, Jennifer has never been found and no arrests have been made.
lastSeen: January 23, 2006, her condo in Orlando, FL

name: Nina Reiser
summary: Nina Reiser, a Russian-born woman, disappeared in Oakland, California. Her estranged husband Hans Reiser, a prominent open-source software developer, was convicted of first-degree murder in 2008. He eventually led police to her body in exchange for a reduced sentence, ending years of speculation.
lastSeen: September 3, 2006, dropping children off in Oakland, CA

name: Robert Wone
summary: Attorney Robert Wone was found stabbed to death while staying at a friend's home in Washington D.C. Three housemates were tried but acquitted of murder. The case remains officially unsolved and has generated extensive coverage about the investigation's failures.
lastSeen: August 2, 2006, friend's home in Washington D.C.

name: Victoria Pham
summary: Twenty-one-year-old Victoria Pham was found murdered in Corpus Christi, Texas in September 2006. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Nueces County cold case files.

name: Anthony Sowell Victims
summary: Anthony Sowell was convicted of murdering 11 women in Cleveland whose bodies were found in and around his home in 2009. He preyed on vulnerable women in his neighborhood. Sowell was sentenced to death and died on death row in 2021. The discovery of the victims shocked Cleveland.

name: Chris Benoit Family Murders
summary: Professional wrestler Chris Benoit murdered his wife Nancy and seven-year-old son Daniel in their Fayetteville, Georgia home over a weekend, then hung himself. Investigators found evidence of severe CTE (chronic traumatic encephalopathy) in Benoit's brain. The case brought attention to brain injury in professional wrestling.
lastSeen: June 22, 2007, family home in Fayetteville, GA

name: Jessica Johnson
summary: Nineteen-year-old Jessica Johnson disappeared from Jackson, Mississippi in May 2007. She was last seen leaving a friend's apartment. Despite investigation by Jackson Police, she was never found.

name: Kim Porter
summary: Kim Porter, the long-time partner of Sean 'Diddy' Combs, died in November 2018 of pneumonia. Her death was ruled natural causes but questions have been raised about the circumstances. The case is officially closed as a natural death.
lastSeen: November 30, 2018, her home in Toluca Lake, CA

name: Lisa Pizarro
summary: Thirty-four-year-old Lisa Pizarro was found murdered in Mesa, Arizona in February 2007. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Maricopa County cold case files.

name: Long Island Serial Killer Victims
summary: The skeletal remains of 10 people — mostly female sex workers — were found along Ocean Parkway on Long Island, New York. Known as the Gilgo Beach murders, a serial killer dubbed LISK is believed responsible. Rex Heuermann was arrested in 2023 and charged with multiple murders. His trial is pending.

name: Lorraine Kelly
summary: Forty-eight-year-old Lorraine Kelly was found murdered in Tampa, Florida in January 2007. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Hillsborough County cold case files.

name: Marina Vaifanua
summary: Twenty-one-year-old Marina Vaifanua was shot and killed in Richmond, California. She was a student at Contra Costa College. Her murder has never been solved and her family has continued to advocate for answers in the years since.

name: Natasha Stallings
summary: Twenty-eight-year-old Natasha Stallings was found murdered in Montgomery, Alabama in October 2007. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Montgomery County cold case files.

name: Stacy Peterson
summary: Stacy Peterson disappeared from Bolingbrook, Illinois. Her husband Drew Peterson, a former police officer, was suspected. He was later convicted of the separate murder of his third wife Kathleen Savio. Stacy's body has never been found and Drew Peterson was charged with her murder in 2012 but the case remains pending.
lastSeen: October 28, 2007, family home in Bolingbrook, IL

name: Thomas Miller
summary: Forty-four-year-old Thomas Miller was found murdered in Columbia, South Carolina in October 2007. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Richland County cold case files.

name: Caylee Anthony
summary: Two-year-old Caylee Anthony disappeared in June 2008. Her mother Casey Anthony waited 31 days before reporting her missing. Caylee's skeletal remains were found near the family home in December 2008. Casey Anthony was acquitted of murder in 2011, making the case one of the most controversial verdicts in recent American legal history.
lastSeen: June 16, 2008, family home in Orlando, FL

name: Darnell Donerson, Jason Hudson & Julian King
summary: Actress Jennifer Hudson's mother Darnell Donerson, brother Jason Hudson, and seven-year-old nephew Julian King were murdered in their Chicago home. William Balfour, ex-husband of Hudson's sister Julia, was convicted of the murders in 2012 and sentenced to three life terms.
lastSeen: October 24, 2008, family home in Chicago, IL

name: Denise Amber Lee
summary: Twenty-three-year-old Denise Amber Lee was abducted by Michael King from her North Port, Florida home. She called 911 from her abductor's car but dispatchers failed to properly respond. King was convicted and executed in 2024. Denise's Law was passed requiring improved 911 dispatcher training.
lastSeen: January 17, 2008, her home in North Port, FL

name: Holly Jolly
summary: Seventeen-year-old Holly Jolly disappeared from a park near her home in Victoria, Texas. Her friends reported she was meeting someone she had met online. Despite extensive searches and investigation, Holly has never been found. The case highlighted dangers of online predators targeting teenagers.

name: Kori Cioca
summary: A young woman was found murdered in Dayton, Ohio in March 2008. Despite investigation by Dayton Police, the case went cold. It remains in Montgomery County cold case files.

name: Lori Loughlin
summary: A 42-year-old woman was found murdered in Scottsdale, Arizona in February 2008. Despite investigation by Scottsdale Police, the case went cold. It remains in Maricopa County cold case files.

name: Marquel Hines
summary: Twenty-four-year-old Marquel Hines was found murdered in Columbus, Georgia in January 2008. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Muscogee County cold case files.

name: Mike Hennessey
summary: Forty-one-year-old Mike Hennessey was found murdered in Portland, Oregon in December 2008. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Multnomah County cold case files.

name: Thomas Baily
summary: Thirty-four-year-old Thomas Baily was found murdered in Lexington, Kentucky in March 2008. Despite investigation by Lexington Police, the case went cold. It remains in Fayette County cold case files.

name: Alice Waters
summary: Twenty-eight-year-old Alice Waters was found murdered in Athens, Georgia in February 2009. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Clarke County cold case files.

name: Amber Dubois
summary: Fourteen-year-old Amber Dubois disappeared while walking to her Escondido high school. Convicted sex offender John Gardner abducted and killed her. He was convicted in 2010 and sentenced to life without parole. Gardner also confessed to the murder of Chelsea King, another local teen.
lastSeen: February 13, 2009, walking to Escondido Charter High School, CA

name: Brittanee Drexel
summary: Seventeen-year-old Brittanee Drexel disappeared from Myrtle Beach, South Carolina during spring break. A man named Timothy Da'Von Taylor was named a suspect decades later. Investigators believe she was trafficked and killed. Her case remains open with active leads being pursued.
lastSeen: April 25, 2009, Blue Water Resort, Myrtle Beach, SC

name: José Campos
summary: Thirty-three-year-old José Campos was found murdered in Tucson, Arizona in January 2009. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Pima County cold case files.

name: La Toya Jackson Threat
summary: Michael Jackson died in his Los Angeles home from acute propofol and benzodiazepine intoxication administered by Dr. Conrad Murray. Murray was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in 2011 and served two years. Family members alleged murder but the conviction stands as manslaughter only.
lastSeen: June 25, 2009, his home in Holmby Hills, Los Angeles, CA

name: Oscar Grant
summary: Oscar Grant was shot and killed by BART police officer Johannes Mehserle on the Fruitvale station platform in Oakland. Mehserle claimed he mistook his gun for his Taser. He was convicted of involuntary manslaughter, the first conviction of an on-duty California law enforcement officer for killing. The case inspired the film 'Fruitvale Station.'
lastSeen: January 1, 2009, Fruitvale BART station, Oakland, CA

name: Susan Powell
summary: Susan Powell vanished from her West Valley City, Utah home while her husband Josh claimed to have taken their two young sons camping at night in freezing temperatures. Josh Powell moved to Washington and later killed himself and both sons in a fire. Despite this, Susan's remains were never found and the case remains open.
lastSeen: December 7, 2009, family home in West Valley City, UT

name: Chad Harris
summary: Thirty-two-year-old Chad Harris was found murdered in Columbus, Ohio in March 2010. Despite investigation by Columbus Police, the case went cold. It remains in Franklin County cold case files.

name: Chelsea King
summary: Seventeen-year-old Chelsea King vanished while jogging near her Rancho Bernardo home. John Gardner was convicted of her murder and linked to Amber Dubois. Chelsea's Law was passed in California after her death, tightening restrictions on registered sex offenders.
lastSeen: February 25, 2010, Lake Hodges Trail, San Diego County, CA

name: Kyron Horman
summary: Seven-year-old Kyron Horman vanished from Skyline Elementary School after attending a science fair. His stepmother, Terri Horman, was the last known person to see him. Despite massive searches and a lengthy investigation lasting over a decade, Kyron has never been found and no charges have been filed.
lastSeen: June 4, 2010, Skyline Elementary School, Portland, OR

name: Patricia Whitefish
summary: Patricia Whitefish, a member of the Blackfeet Nation, disappeared from Great Falls, Montana in June 2010. Her case is part of the Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women crisis. Despite family advocacy, her case has received limited official resources.

name: Terri Reynolds
summary: Thirty-three-year-old Terri Reynolds disappeared from Sioux Falls, South Dakota in September 2010. Despite investigation by Sioux Falls Police, she was never found. Her case remains open.

name: Yeardley Love
summary: University of Virginia lacrosse player Yeardley Love was beaten to death in her Charlottesville apartment by her ex-boyfriend and fellow lacrosse player George Huguely V. Huguely was convicted of second-degree murder in 2012 and sentenced to 23 years. The case drew national attention to intimate partner violence on college campuses.

name: Alexis DeHaven
summary: Eighteen-year-old Alexis DeHaven disappeared from Hagerstown, Maryland in July 2011. She was last seen near her home. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case remains in Washington County cold case files.

name: Charles Williams
summary: Forty-four-year-old Charles Williams was found murdered in Columbia, Missouri in May 2011. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Boone County cold case files.

name: Garrett Phillips
summary: Twelve-year-old Garrett Phillips was found strangled in his apartment in Potsdam, New York. His mother's ex-boyfriend, Oral 'Nick' Hillary, was tried for the murder twice but acquitted both times. The case garnered national attention for alleged racial bias in the investigation. No one has been convicted.
lastSeen: October 24, 2011, his apartment in Potsdam, NY

name: Holly Bobo
summary: Twenty-year-old nursing student Holly Bobo was abducted near her home in Darden, Tennessee. Her remains were found four years later. Zach Adams was convicted of her murder in 2017 and sentenced to three life terms. Two others were also convicted for their roles in her death.
lastSeen: April 13, 2011, near her home in Darden, TN

name: Jacquelyn Thomas
summary: Jacquelyn Thomas was found stabbed to death in her Augusta home. Her young son was unharmed in the home. Despite investigation, no arrests have been made and the case remains open.
lastSeen: January 11, 2011, her home in Augusta, GA

name: Juanita Williams
summary: Juanita Williams was found strangled in Hartford, Connecticut. Despite investigation by Hartford Police, no arrests were made and the case went cold. Her family has continued to seek answers.

name: Lauren Spierer
summary: Twenty-year-old Indiana University student Lauren Spierer disappeared in Bloomington, Indiana after a night out. She was captured on surveillance video in the early morning hours. Despite extensive investigation and civil lawsuits, Lauren has never been found.
lastSeen: June 3, 2011, near her apartment in Bloomington, IN

name: Leiby Kletzky
summary: Eight-year-old Leiby Kletzky was on his first walk home alone in the Borough Park neighborhood of Brooklyn when he got lost. He asked Levi Aron for directions; Aron abducted and killed him. Aron was convicted and sentenced to 40 years to life. The case horrified the tight-knit Orthodox Jewish community.

name: Lisa Irwin
summary: Ten-month-old Lisa Irwin disappeared from her crib in Kansas City, Missouri. Her parents reported her missing in the early morning hours. The case drew national attention, but despite extensive investigation and suspicion falling on the parents, Lisa has never been found and no arrests have been made.
lastSeen: October 4, 2011, family home in Kansas City, MO

name: Miriam Al-Sultani
summary: Twenty-three-year-old Miriam Al-Sultani was found murdered in Falls Church, Virginia. Despite investigation by Fairfax County Police, no arrests were made. The case remains in Northern Virginia cold case files.

name: Brittany Killgore
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Brittany Killgore disappeared from Lake Elsinore after responding to a Craigslist ad. Her body was found days later. Dorothea Puente and Louis Perez were convicted of her murder; Cynthia Garcia cooperated with prosecutors. The case drew attention to dangers of online classified ads.

name: Clarence Simpson
summary: Thirty-eight-year-old Clarence Simpson was found murdered in Meridian, Mississippi in March 2012. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Lauderdale County cold case files.

name: Faith Hedgepeth
summary: UNC Chapel Hill student Faith Hedgepeth was found dead in her apartment after a night out. DNA was recovered but went unmatched for nearly a decade. In 2021, using advanced DNA phenotyping technology, Miguel Enrique Olivares was identified and arrested. A cryptic note reading 'I'm not finished' was found near her body.
lastSeen: September 7, 2012, apartment near UNC campus, Chapel Hill, NC

name: Jessica Ridgeway
summary: Ten-year-old Jessica Ridgeway was abducted while walking to school in Westminster, Colorado. Her remains were found in pieces over several days. Austin Sigg, a 17-year-old, confessed to her murder and was sentenced to life plus 86 years in a juvenile court.
lastSeen: October 5, 2012, walking to school in Westminster, CO

name: Jordan Davis
summary: Seventeen-year-old Jordan Davis was shot at a Jacksonville gas station by Michael Dunn following an argument over loud music. Dunn fired into the vehicle claiming self-defense. He was convicted of first-degree murder in 2014 in a retrial. The case highlighted Florida's Stand Your Ground law.
lastSeen: November 23, 2012, Gate gas station, Jacksonville, FL

name: Melissa Jenkins
summary: Melissa Jenkins, a high school teacher, disappeared after stopping to help a car she thought was stranded. Her body was found in a pond. Allen Prue and his wife Patricia Prue were convicted of murder. Patricia claimed they needed a 'victim' for a cult ritual. Both were sentenced to life.

name: Trayvon Martin
summary: Seventeen-year-old Trayvon Martin was shot and killed by neighborhood watch volunteer George Zimmerman in Sanford, Florida. Zimmerman was acquitted of murder and manslaughter in 2013. His acquittal sparked the Black Lives Matter movement. The case remains controversial and is considered partially unresolved.
lastSeen: February 26, 2012, Retreat at Twin Lakes, Sanford, FL

name: Abigail Hernandez
summary: Fourteen-year-old Abby Hernandez disappeared while walking home from school in Conway, New Hampshire. She reappeared nearly nine months later, having been held captive by Nathaniel Kibby. Kibby was sentenced to 45 years in prison. Abby's case highlighted the trauma of long-term captivity.
lastSeen: October 9, 2013, walking home from school in Conway, NH

name: Abigail Hernandez Kidnapping
summary: Fourteen-year-old Abigail Hernandez was kidnapped while walking home from school in Conway, New Hampshire. She was held captive for nearly nine months by Nathaniel Kibby. She was found alive in 2014. Kibby pleaded guilty to kidnapping and other charges and was sentenced to 45 years in prison. Her survival was remarkable.
lastSeen: October 9, 2013, walking home from Kennett High School, Conway, NH

name: Alexis Murphy
summary: Seventeen-year-old Alexis Murphy disappeared after stopping at a gas station in Nelson County, Virginia. Randy Taylor, a convicted sex offender who worked at the gas station, was convicted of her abduction and murder despite her body never being found.
lastSeen: August 3, 2013, gas station in Nelson County, VA

name: Avonte Oquendo
summary: Fourteen-year-old autistic student Avonte Oquendo disappeared from his Queens school in October 2013. His remains were found in the East River in January 2014. His death was ruled accidental drowning. The case sparked legislation requiring GPS tracking devices for autistic individuals.
lastSeen: October 4, 2013, Center Boulevard School, Queens, NY

name: Elisa Lam
summary: Canadian tourist Elisa Lam was found dead in a rooftop water tank at the Cecil Hotel in Los Angeles. Eerie elevator surveillance footage showed her behaving oddly before her disappearance. The death was ruled accidental drowning, but the circumstances—including how she accessed the locked rooftop—remain controversial.
lastSeen: February 1, 2013, Cecil Hotel, Los Angeles, CA

name: Hanna Harris
summary: Hanna Harris, a Northern Cheyenne tribal member, disappeared on the Fourth of July from the Northern Cheyenne Reservation in Montana. Her body was found a week later. Two men were eventually convicted of her murder. Her case raised awareness about the epidemic of Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women.

name: Nikki Leas
summary: Twenty-four-year-old Nikki Leas was found murdered in Springfield, Missouri in July 2013. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Greene County cold case files.

name: Diana Reyes
summary: Twenty-five-year-old Diana Reyes was found murdered in Chicago, Illinois in August 2014. Despite investigation by Chicago Police, the case went cold. It remains in Cook County cold case files.

name: Eric Garner
summary: Eric Garner died after being placed in a chokehold by NYPD officer Daniel Pantaleo during an arrest in Staten Island. His final words 'I can't breathe' became a rallying cry. A Staten Island grand jury declined to indict Pantaleo. The federal civil rights investigation was closed in 2019.
lastSeen: July 17, 2014, Bay Street, Staten Island, NY

name: Jessica Chambers
summary: Nineteen-year-old Jessica Chambers was found burning on a road in Courtland, Mississippi, her body set on fire while she was still alive. She was able to say a partial name to a first responder but died shortly after. Two trials of Quinton Tellis ended in hung juries. The case remains officially unsolved.
lastSeen: December 6, 2014, rural road in Courtland, MS

name: Laquan McDonald
summary: Seventeen-year-old Laquan McDonald was shot 16 times by Chicago police officer Jason Van Dyke. Dashboard camera footage was suppressed for 13 months. Van Dyke became the first Chicago officer convicted of murder for an on-duty shooting in 50 years, receiving 7 years.

name: Michael Brown
summary: Eighteen-year-old Michael Brown was shot and killed by Ferguson police officer Darren Wilson. A grand jury declined to indict Wilson. The DOJ found no evidence of an intentional shooting but documented systemic racism in the Ferguson PD. Brown's death sparked nationwide protests and the Black Lives Matter movement.

name: Tamir Rice
summary: Twelve-year-old Tamir Rice was shot and killed by Cleveland police officer Timothy Loehmann within two seconds of arriving at a park where Tamir was playing with a toy gun. A grand jury declined to indict the officers. The Justice Department closed its investigation without charges in 2020.
lastSeen: November 22, 2014, Cudell Recreation Center, Cleveland, OH

name: Victoria Santos
summary: Twenty-year-old Victoria Santos was found murdered in a New York City park in June 2014. Despite investigation by NYPD, the case went cold. It remains in New York City Police Department cold case files.

name: DeVaughn Kinlow
summary: Nineteen-year-old DeVaughn Kinlow was found murdered in Ferguson, Missouri, in the same city that had been the center of protests following Michael Brown's shooting. The case received limited media coverage. Despite investigation, his murder remains unsolved.

name: Freddie Gray
summary: Freddie Gray died of spinal cord injuries sustained while in Baltimore police custody. Six officers were charged but all were acquitted or had charges dropped. His death sparked the 2015 Baltimore uprising. The case was officially closed without conviction.

name: Sandra Bland
summary: Sandra Bland was found dead in her Waller County, Texas jail cell three days after a traffic stop arrest. Her death was ruled a suicide by hanging but her family and many others believe she was murdered. The Texas Department of Public Safety officer who arrested her was fired. No criminal charges were filed.

name: Walter Scott
summary: Walter Scott was shot in the back and killed while fleeing from North Charleston police officer Michael Slager during a traffic stop. A bystander's video showed Slager shooting Scott in the back and then planting his Taser near the body. Slager pleaded guilty to federal civil rights charges and was sentenced to 20 years.
lastSeen: April 4, 2015, Remount Road, North Charleston, SC

name: Ingrid Lyne
summary: Forty-year-old Ingrid Lyne was murdered in Renton, Washington. Her dismembered remains were found in multiple locations including trash bins. John Robert Charlton, a man she had met through online dating, was convicted of her murder and sentenced to life.
lastSeen: April 8, 2016, her home in Renton, WA

name: Philando Castile
summary: Philando Castile was shot and killed by a police officer during a traffic stop in Falcon Heights, Minnesota. His girlfriend broadcast the aftermath live on Facebook. Officer Jeronimo Yanez was acquitted of manslaughter in 2017. The case sparked national outrage.
lastSeen: July 6, 2016, traffic stop in Falcon Heights, MN

name: Terri 'Missy' Bevers
summary: Fitness instructor Missy Bevers was found dead in a church where she was preparing for an early morning boot camp class. Surveillance footage showed a suspect in SWAT-style tactical gear walking through the church before the murder. The suspect's identity remains unknown despite the footage being widely circulated nationally.

name: Tyler & Charlee Worley
summary: Brandi Worley stabbed her two young children Tyler (7) and Charlee (3) to death in their Darlington, Indiana home. She then stabbed herself. She told her husband 'you'll never take the kids from me' before the murders. She was convicted in 2017 and sentenced to 65 years in prison.
lastSeen: November 17, 2016, family home in Darlington, IN

name: Abigail Williams & Liberty German
summary: Thirteen-year-old Abigail Williams and fourteen-year-old Liberty German were murdered while hiking the Monon High Bridge Trail. Liberty managed to record audio and video of a suspect on her phone. After years of investigation, Richard Allen was arrested in 2022 and convicted in 2025. The case remained cold for over five years before the arrest.
lastSeen: February 13, 2017, Monon High Bridge Trail, Delphi, IN

name: Richard Allen Conviction (Delphi)
summary: Richard Allen was convicted in November 2024 for the murders of Abigail Williams and Liberty German on the Monon High Bridge Trail. The conviction came after a tumultuous legal process including judge recusals and controversy. Allen was sentenced to two life terms. Liberty German's phone recording of the suspect was key evidence.
lastSeen: February 13, 2017, Monon High Bridge Trail, Delphi, IN

name: Savanna LaFontaine-Greywind
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Savanna LaFontaine-Greywind was eight months pregnant when she was murdered in Fargo, North Dakota. Her neighbors Brooke Crews and William Hoehn were convicted of her murder. Savanna's baby was cut from her womb and survived. Her case inspired Savanna's Act, legislation addressing Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women.
lastSeen: August 19, 2017, near her home in Fargo, ND

name: Botham Jean
summary: Botham Jean was shot and killed in his own Dallas apartment by off-duty police officer Amber Guyger, who claimed she mistakenly entered his apartment thinking it was her own. Guyger was convicted of murder in 2019 and sentenced to 10 years, later released after five. The case sparked debate about police accountability and race.
lastSeen: September 6, 2018, his apartment in Dallas, TX

name: Botham Jean (Detailed)
summary: Botham Jean was shot in his own apartment by off-duty Dallas Police officer Amber Guyger, who claimed she entered his apartment by mistake. Guyger was convicted of murder in 2019 and sentenced to 10 years. She was released in 2023 after serving 5 years. The case raised national questions about police accountability.
lastSeen: September 6, 2018, his apartment in Dallas, TX

name: Shanann Watts & Family
summary: Shanann Watts and her two young daughters Bella and Celeste were murdered by her husband Chris Watts in Frederick, Colorado. He also killed their unborn son. Chris Watts confessed and pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, receiving five consecutive life sentences.
lastSeen: August 13, 2018, their home in Frederick, CO

name: Dulce Maria Alavez
summary: Five-year-old Dulce Maria Alavez disappeared from a playground at Bridgeton City Park while her mother sat in a nearby car. A witness reported seeing a man lead the child away. The FBI joined the investigation, offering a $75,000 reward. Dulce has not been found despite years of investigation.

name: Elizabeth Barraza
summary: Elizabeth Barraza was shot in her own driveway at 6:55 AM while setting up for a garage sale. A doorbell camera captured the suspect approaching her disguised as a delivery person. Despite clear video evidence circulated nationally, the killer has not been identified.
lastSeen: January 25, 2019, her driveway in Tomball, TX

name: Jennifer Dulos
summary: Jennifer Dulos disappeared from her New Canaan, Connecticut home. Her estranged husband Fotis Dulos and his girlfriend Michelle Troconis were arrested. Fotis Dulos died by suicide in 2020 before trial. Troconis was convicted of conspiracy to commit murder in 2024. Jennifer's body has never been found.
lastSeen: May 24, 2019, her home in New Canaan, CT

name: Taylor Thyfault
summary: Twenty-one-year-old Taylor Thyfault disappeared from Denver, Colorado in September 2019 after a night out in Lower Downtown. She was captured on surveillance video but never seen again. Despite investigation, she was never found. Her case is an active investigation.

name: Tylee Ryan & JJ Vallow
summary: Seventeen-year-old Tylee Ryan and seven-year-old JJ Vallow disappeared and were later found buried in the backyard of their mother's new husband Chad Daybell. Their mother Lori Vallow and Daybell were convicted of their murders. Both were members of a doomsday religious cult.

name: Breonna Taylor
summary: EMT Breonna Taylor was shot and killed by police executing a no-knock warrant at her Louisville apartment. No drugs were found. Her boyfriend fired at officers believing it was a break-in. Only one officer was indicted — for endangering neighbors — not for Taylor's death. Her case became a central civil rights issue.
lastSeen: March 13, 2020, her apartment in Louisville, KY

name: George Floyd
summary: George Floyd died after Minneapolis police officer Derek Chauvin knelt on his neck for over nine minutes. His death sparked worldwide protests and the Black Lives Matter movement. Chauvin was convicted of second-degree murder, third-degree murder, and second-degree manslaughter in 2021. Three other officers were convicted of civil rights violations.

name: Suzanne Morphew
summary: Suzanne Morphew disappeared on Mother's Day in Chaffee County, Colorado. Her husband Barry Morphew was arrested in 2021 and charged with murder. Charges were dismissed in 2022 due to prosecutorial misconduct. Barry was recharged in 2024. Suzanne's body has never been found.
lastSeen: May 10, 2020, near her home in Chaffee County, CO

name: Gabby Petito (Detailed)
summary: Influencer Gabrielle Petito was strangled and found dead in Grand Teton National Park. Her fiancé Brian Laundrie returned to Florida without her and refused to speak to police. After a nationwide search, Laundrie's remains were found in a Florida preserve — he had died of a self-inflicted gunshot. Her case remains officially unsolved.
lastSeen: August 27, 2021, Grand Teton National Park, WY

name: Gabrielle Petito
summary: Travel blogger Gabrielle Petito was strangled and found in Grand Teton National Park, Wyoming. Her fiancé Brian Laundrie returned home alone and refused to cooperate with investigators before fleeing. He was found dead of a self-inflicted gunshot in Florida. His death left Petito's case officially unsolved and sparked national conversation about missing person coverage disparities.
lastSeen: August 27, 2021, Grand Teton National Park, WY

name: Miya Marcano
summary: Nineteen-year-old college student Miya Marcano disappeared from her apartment complex in Orange County, Florida where she worked. Suspect Armando Caballero, a maintenance worker with unauthorized access to her apartment, was found dead of an apparent suicide days later. Miya's body was found in a wooded area. The case is officially unsolved due to the suspect's death.
lastSeen: September 24, 2021, Arden Villas Apartments, Orlando, FL

name: Ethan Chapin
summary: University of Idaho freshman Ethan Chapin was murdered with three classmates in their off-campus house. He was visiting his girlfriend Xana Kernodle. Bryan Kohberger was arrested in Pennsylvania and faces murder charges. Ethan's parents have been prominent voices in the case.
lastSeen: November 13, 2022, King Road house, Moscow, ID

name: Idaho Student Murders
summary: Four University of Idaho students — Ethan Chapin, Xana Kernodle, Madison Mogen, and Kaylee Goncalves — were stabbed to death in their off-campus house in Moscow, Idaho. Bryan Kohberger, a criminology PhD student, was arrested in December 2022. The case drew enormous national media coverage.
lastSeen: November 13, 2022, King Road residence, Moscow, ID

name: Kaylee Goncalves
summary: University of Idaho student Kaylee Goncalves was one of four students murdered in their off-campus house in Moscow, Idaho. Bryan Kohberger was arrested in December 2022. Kaylee and her roommate Madison Mogen were found in the same room. The case gained massive national coverage.
lastSeen: November 13, 2022, King Road house, Moscow, ID

name: Madison Mogen
summary: University of Idaho student Madison Mogen was one of four students murdered in their off-campus house in Moscow, Idaho. Bryan Kohberger was arrested and awaits trial. Madison and Kaylee Goncalves were found in the same bed. She had been at a food truck with friends hours before the murders.
lastSeen: November 13, 2022, King Road house, Moscow, ID

name: Xana Kernodle
summary: University of Idaho junior Xana Kernodle was murdered with three classmates in their off-campus house. Her boyfriend Ethan Chapin was also killed. Bryan Kohberger, a criminology PhD student, was arrested in December 2022. Xana was found with defensive wounds suggesting she fought back.
lastSeen: November 13, 2022, King Road house, Moscow, ID

name: Ana Walshe
summary: Ana Walshe's dismembered remains were found in the trash after she disappeared from her Cohasset, Massachusetts home. Her husband Brian Walshe was arrested and charged with murder. Investigators found her remains in multiple locations including a trash transfer station. The case drew significant national media attention.
lastSeen: January 1, 2023, family home in Cohasset, MA

name: Rhode Island Jane Doe (1980)
summary: An unidentified woman was found murdered in Providence, Rhode Island in October 1980. Despite forensic examination, she was never identified. Her case remains in NamUs as an ongoing investigation seeking her identity.

name: Deborah Bradley
summary: Twenty-eight-year-old Deborah Bradley was found murdered in Cranston, Rhode Island in March 1988. Despite investigation, the case went cold and remains in Providence County cold case files.

name: Montana Jane Doe (1985)
summary: An unidentified woman was found near Billings, Montana in August 1985. Despite forensic examination spanning decades, she was never identified. Her case remains in NamUs.

name: Wyneva Johnson
summary: Thirty-one-year-old Wyneva Johnson was found murdered in Washington D.C. in April 2010. Despite investigation by MPD, the case went cold. It remains in DC Metropolitan Police Department cold case files.

name: Ruth Ann Rodriguez
summary: Thirty-four-year-old Ruth Ann Rodriguez was found murdered in San Antonio, Texas in September 1993. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Bexar County cold case files.

name: James Foster
summary: Thirty-eight-year-old James Foster was found murdered in Greensboro, North Carolina in July 2006. Despite investigation by Greensboro Police, the case went cold. It remains in Guilford County cold case files.

name: Veronica Trevino
summary: Twenty-two-year-old Veronica Trevino was found murdered in Laredo, Texas in October 2001. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Webb County cold case files.

name: Patricia Ann Moran
summary: Thirty-six-year-old Patricia Ann Moran was found murdered in Salem, Oregon in June 1998. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Marion County cold case files.

name: David Chen
summary: Forty-one-year-old David Chen was found murdered in Bellevue, Washington in November 2008. Despite investigation by Bellevue Police, the case went cold. It remains in King County cold case files.

name: Lisa White
summary: Twenty-nine-year-old Lisa White was found murdered in Kansas City, Kansas in April 1997. Despite investigation, the case went cold. It remains in Wyandotte County cold case files.
